technicalAnalysis.title: Pilih Bab
technicalAnalysis.subtitle: Kuasai seni membaca grafik, mengenali pola, dan memahami volume untuk membuat keputusan investasi yang tepat.

chapter1.title: Bab 1: Pola Grafik
chapter1.description: Cara identifikasi perubahan tren dan menentukan target profit.
chapter1.content_title: Jenis-Jenis Chart Pattern untuk Trading Saham

bullish.title: Pola Grafik Bullish
bullish.description: Pola grafik bullish berarti pattern yang jika terbentuk, maka harga saham berpotensi untuk naik.

paragraphs: Pattern ini berbentuk seperti segitiga siku-siku dengan posisi garis di bagian bawah bergerak menyamping ke atas.
Sementara di bagian atas mendatar yang digambarkan dengan resistance atau di pattern ini lebih dikenal dengan neckline.
Pattern ini bisa dikatakan valid atau terkonfirmasi, ketika harga berhasil melewati garis lurusnya atau disebut dengan neckline.
Sementara itu target harga atau target price dapat diukur setara dengan jarak antara neckline dan garis yang menyamping ketika pertama kali terbentuk.

text: Perhatikan pergerakan harga BBNI semenjak 15 Juni 2021.
Ketika harga BBNI terjun ke bawah, dia tertahan di satu titik, yaitu di 4500, dimana ini berfungsi sebagai support.
Nah, selanjutnya ketika BBNI rebound, dia membentuk sebuah garis lurus atau yang dinamakan dengan resistance yang berfungsi sebagai neckline.

Neckline ini merupakan titik konfirmasi dari pergerakan harga BBNI yang sedang dalam pembentukan pattern ascending triangle, dimana harga sahamnya semakin lama membuat low yang tidak lebih low daripada titik sebelumnya.
Ketika harga menembus areal neckline, inilah yang dinamakan dengan konfirmasi.
Dengan terbentuknya bentuk chart ini, kita bisa tetapkan target kenaikan dari BBNI yaitu sepanjang pergerakan ascending triangle-nya, yaitu dari titik terbawah ke garis neckline.
Kita bisa lihat dari chart di atas bahwa BBNI berhasil mencapai target kenaikannya.

paragraphs: This pattern resembles two shoulders and one head, but inverted.
The two shoulders are nearly equal in size, while the head is the largest or has the deepest decline.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern occurs when the price successfully breaks through the neckline.
Once confirmed, we can determine the price target based on the distance between the lowest point of the head and the neckline.

text: When the ISAT share price began to correct on March 26, 2019, it rebounded, then reached its last high at 2700, creating a resistance point.

Note that ISAT then fell, forming a deeper trough. When ISAT rebounded again, it was again held back by the resistance area previously created by the highest trough. Therefore, when we draw a straight line, we create a resistance or supply area called an Inverted Head and Shoulders.
This is what is called a neckline, which is the first shoulder movement, followed by a head, and finally forming a head and shoulders.
When the ISAT price successfully breaks through the neckline, this is what is called pattern confirmation.
The target movement of ISAT can be calculated along the movement from the trough, namely from the bottom to the neckline.
Then, note that ISAT successfully touched the target area of ​​the inverted head and shoulders movement, then it corrected and was unable to continue its upward movement.

paragraphs: This pattern resembles a cup and its handle. Ideally, the handle should not be larger than the cup. Both look like a large valley and a small valley, similar to a cup.
The valley in the cup appears steeper than the valley in the handle. This is because the lowest point on the handle is higher than the lowest point on the cup.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern is when the price successfully breaks out or passes the cup boundary.
After that, we can determine the target price based on the distance between the lowest point of the cup and the neckline or cup boundary, which is essentially the furthest or highest point.

text: The rules of a cup and handle call for a very deep valley. Here, we see WIKA's price movement correcting, even breaking through its last low, then rebounding to reach the resistance area, the highest point on July 22nd, thus creating a supply area, also known as the neckline.
Then, WIKA's correction formed a higher low, meaning it wasn't lower than the last low, so WIKA's shape resembled a cup.
When WIKA's share price successfully broke through the neckline, this was called confirmation.
With this chart pattern formed, we can set a price increase target by drawing from the lowest point to the neckline, then moving this movement to the neckline area.
Then, we can see from the chart above that WIKA successfully achieved its increase target.

paragraphs: This pattern resembles two adjacent or side-by-side valleys, and it's one of the most common.
Ideally, the lowest points are at the same level.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern is when the price successfully breaks through the neckline.
After that, we can determine the target price by using the distance between the lowest point of one of the bottoms and the neckline, which is usually the same low point.

text: Let's look at the two lowest points (pivot lows) of ADHI stock, namely at 660.
Then, far above that, ADHI formed a resistance line, which was ADHI's last high before forming the second bottom.
Now, this area is what we call the neckline if we draw a straight line.
As of August 20, 2021, ADHI cannot be considered to have formed a double bottom pattern until the neckline area is broken, which is what is called confirmation.
With the formation of this chart pattern, we can set a price increase target by drawing from the lowest point to the neckline, then moving this movement to the neckline area.
Then, we can see from the chart above that ADHI has successfully achieved its increase target.

paragraphs: This pattern begins with the formation of a pole, which indicates a rapid price increase, followed by the formation of a flag.
The flag forms after the pole forms and can be identified by price movement that resembles a downward-sloping rectangle.
A flag is simply a box formed by a channel line in a downtrend.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern occurs when the price successfully breaks through the rectangular area formed by the flag.
Traders can then determine the target price using the height of the previously formed pole.

text: We can see that on September 21, 2021, there was a massive upward movement.
It then corrected, forming a flag or a downward-sloping rectangle. When this rectangle area is successfully broken upward, it serves as a confirmation area.
This is a retracement period, or resting period, for a stock experiencing a trend.

Then, we can see that ADRO successfully confirmed the continuation of the bullish flag, meaning it continued its uptrend. To calculate the target, we can use the pole area of ​​the bullish flag, which is from September 21 to October 6.
Therefore, ADRO successfully achieved its upside target.

paragraphs: This pattern resembles a flagpole, similar to a bullish flag, but this time the flag is triangular instead of square, or what is commonly known as a pennant.
The pole is formed from a significant surge in stock prices over a short period of time.
Meanwhile, the flag, or pennant, is formed from price movements with increasingly higher support points and lower resistance points.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern is when the price successfully moves past the upper line of the triangle.
After that, traders can determine the target price using the height of the previously formed pole.

text: We can see that on May 19, 2020, there was a massive upward movement.
It then corrected, forming a pennant or triangle.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern is when the price successfully breaks through the diagonal resistance of the pennant.
After that, traders can determine the target price using the height of the previously formed pole.

paragraphs: This pattern resembles a wedge-shaped shoe often worn to parties.
This pattern can be identified by the increasingly narrow distance between the high and low points.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern is when the price successfully moves past the upper line of the wedge triangle.
After that, we can determine the price target by using the distance between the highest and lowest wedge areas at the initial formation area.

text: We can see that the stock's movement consistently breaks through lower lows.
This movement creates a downward trendline.
Furthermore, at the top, it also creates a downward trendline, forming a wedge-like shape.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern is when the price successfully breaks through its resistance (the upper line of the wedge).
After that, traders can determine the target price by measuring the largest gap between the two lines, which is the starting point of the wedge's formation.
Then, we measure the upward target from the breakout point.

bearish.title: Bearish Chart Pattern
bearish.description: A bearish chart pattern means a pattern that, if formed, indicates a potential drop in stock prices. The purpose of understanding this pattern is to be prepared before the price reaches its target decline or to project how far the price will fall.

paragraphs: This pattern resembles a right-angled triangle with a downward-pointing trendline and a neckline (support) below it.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern occurs when the price successfully falls below the neckline, a process known as a breakdown.
Just like an ascending triangle, we can determine the price target by measuring the distance between the neckline and the sideline when it first forms.

text: UNTR's peak was at 40312, and note that when UNTR corrected, it created a support area.
When we draw an equation from 40312, it corrected and then rebounded. UNTR's rebound failed to create a new high, so it made a movement that continued to make higher lows.
When we draw a trend line, it will create a downward trend line.
This downward trend line is already a sign that the stock price is weakening.
When we look at the neckline below, this is a movement called a descending triangle.
When the stock price breaks through the neckline, this is what is called confirmation.
With the formation of this chart pattern, we can set a downside target, which is from the top point to the neckline.
We can see from the chart above that UNTR successfully achieved the downside target of its bearish pattern.

paragraphs: This pattern resembles a head with two shoulders.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern occurs when the price successfully falls below the neckline (breakdown).
The neckline can be drawn by drawing a line from the low point formed by each head and shoulder.
Traders can determine price targets by measuring the distance from the head to the neckline.
This pattern resembles an inverted cup with a handle.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern occurs when the price successfully falls below its neckline (breakdown).
Afterward, traders can determine the target price based on the greatest distance between the top of the cup and the neckline, as shown in the cup above.
This pattern resembles two mountains. It occurs when the stock price fails to break through the previous high.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern is when the price successfully falls below its neckline (breakdown).
We can think of the neckline as the lower boundary or foot of a mountain.
Traders can then determine the target price decline based on the distance between the peak and the foot of the mountain.
This pattern begins with a sharp price decline and is identified by price movement forming an upward-sloping rectangular pattern.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern is when the price successfully falls from below the flag (breakdown).
Afterward, traders can determine a price target using the height of the previously formed pole.
This pattern can be identified when there is a sudden or abrupt price drop, followed by a price reversal.
In this pattern, the steep price drop creates a flagpole.
Then, when the price experiences sideways movement or consolidation, it forms a triangle pattern as its pennant.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern is when the price successfully falls below the pennant or a breakdown occurs.
After that, we can determine the price target using the height of the previously formed flagpole.

sideways.title: Sideways Chart Pattern
sideways.description: A sideways chart pattern means a pattern that has no clear direction, but has potential to rise or fall.

paragraphs: This pattern resembles an equilateral triangle that gradually tapers.
This pattern typically forms when the price fails to form a lower low or a higher high.
The triangle in this pattern forms because the high and low points become closer together over time.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern occurs when the price successfully falls below or rises above the triangle, or experiences a breakout or breakdown.
Afterward, determine the target price using the distance between the high and low of the triangle when it first formed.

text: We can see that the stock price has moved sideways for quite a long time.
This sideways movement creates a symmetrical triangle pattern.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern occurs when the price successfully falls below or rises above the triangle, or experiences a breakout or breakdown.
Afterward, determine the target price using the distance between the high and low of the triangle when it first formed.

paragraphs: As the name suggests, this pattern resembles a rectangle.
In this pattern, the price forms a sideways pattern with resistance and support points that are nearly parallel to the previous points (the numbers are almost the same).
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern is when the price successfully falls below (breaks down) or rises above (breaks out) the rectangle.
After that, we can determine the price target based on the height of the rectangle.

text: We can see that the stock price has moved sideways for quite a long time.
This sideways movement creates a rectangle pattern.
Confirmation of the validity of this pattern is when the price successfully falls below (breaks down) or rises above (breaks out) the rectangle.
Afterward, determine the price target based on the height of the rectangle.

false_break.title: False Break
false_break.description: A false break can be defined as a failed breakout, where resistance remains strong, causing the price, which had previously broken through, to fall back below its resistance.

paragraphs: After understanding chart patterns and projecting stock price movements after a pattern forms, it's helpful to understand the term false break.
A false break can be defined as a failed breakout, where resistance remains strong, causing the price, which had previously broken through, to fall back below its resistance.

This also applies to a false breakdown, which can be defined as a failed breakdown, where support remains strong, causing the price, which had previously broken through, to rise back above its support.

text: First, we can see that WIKA's price has formed a double bottom pattern.
This means that the price has touched a low point twice, and then rebounded.
However, after the price broke through the resistance of the double bottom pattern, it failed to hold above it.
This is called a false breakout.
This can happen because the resistance is still strong, or because there is not enough buying volume to push the price higher.
As a result, the price fell back below the resistance, and the double bottom pattern was invalidated.

paragraphs: In conclusion, chart patterns serve as signals for traders to take positions before prices rise, or to sell a stock before the price drops further.
Furthermore, to support or confirm a breakout from a pattern, support, or resistance level, we must also look at volume.
This is because there's a possibility that the chart pattern formed is invalid, leading to a false breakout.

chapter2.title: Bab 2: Volume
chapter2.description: Cara validasi perubahan pergerakan tren harga saham.
chapter2.content_title: Cara Analisis Volume saat Trading Saham

paragraphs: Indikator volume menunjukkan besar kecilnya jumlah transaksi pada suatu saham.
Analisis volume itu hal yang tidak bisa dipisahkan dalam melakukan analisa teknikal karena volume merupakan kunci dari pergerakan harga.
Price movements without being based on volume can be said to be useless.
The main use of volume is to confirm the strength of the ongoing trend and also confirm the price movements that occur, such as chart patterns, resistance breakouts, and support breakdowns.
Because of its confirmatory nature, the volume indicator cannot be used alone, but is used to complement other analyses.
Since volume is an indicator of the number of stock transactions, a higher volume indicates a greater number of transactions.
Conversely, a lower volume indicates a smaller number of transactions.
As an indicator, high volume indicates positive confirmation of the current price movement.
This can also be interpreted as meaning that high volume means the trend or price movement is strong or has power.
Conversely, low volume indicates that the price movement is not "well-confirmed" or lacks power.
Therefore, it is highly likely that the current trend or price movement will not last long, could turn into a reversal, or even a false break.
In an ongoing trend, volume can be a supporting indicator to determine the strength of the ongoing trend.
By looking at the volume indicator, we can determine whether the trend will continue, weaken, or has the potential to reverse.
Increasing or high volume indicates that the trend still has the power to continue. Meanwhile, low volume indicates that the trend is weakening and will soon end or reverse.

points: Bullish Trend + Increasing/Large Volume → Confirmation of Trend Continuation
Bullish Trend + Decreasing/Large Volume → Trend Weakening/Potential for Reversal
Bearish Trend + Increasing/Large Volume → Confirmation of Trend Continuation
Bearish Trend + Decreasing/Large Volume → Trend Weakening/Potential for Reversal

paragraphs: When the price breaks through resistance or support, also known as a breakout or breakdown, volume can be an indicator of whether the breakout is valid or not.
By looking at the volume indicator, we can determine whether the breakout is valid and has the potential to continue, or whether it is a false breakout and a reversal.
For example, if the price successfully breaks through resistance and is supported by relatively large volume, it is considered a valid breakout, and the price is likely to continue rising.

points.text: Breakout + High Volume → Valid and potential for further upside.
Breakout + Low Volume → No power, potential for a false breakout.
Breakdown + High Volume → Valid and potential for further downside.
Breakdown + Low Volume → No power, potential for a false breakout.

content.p1: Volume is also used to confirm chart patterns. For example, price confirmation in the Cup and Handle pattern.

example.title: Example of Volume Confirmation in Cup & Handle Pattern

text: When a 'Cup' forms, the price moves down, accompanied by low volume.
However, when the price rises, the volume is greater than when it falls.
If the price successfully breaks through the neckline, we can expect the highest volume compared to previous volumes.
If these three things occur, it can be said that the volume confirms a change in the trend of the chart pattern, or the signal from the pattern is valid.

content.p2: In the Cup and Handle pattern, volume is expected to decrease during the formation of the cup and increase during the formation of the handle.
content.p3: This indicates that the price is consolidating and the trend is weakening, then the price breaks through resistance with increased volume, indicating that the trend is strengthening.

list.title: Volume confirmation in the formation of a bullish pattern:

points.text: Small volume when the price corrects
Large volume when the price rises
Volume confirmation in the formation of a bullish pattern

paragraphs: The high or low volume is actually a subjective assessment.
However, a basic rule of thumb is that high volume occurs when volume is above the 20-day moving average.

text: Tips: Use the MA20 Volume indicator

chapter3.title: Bab 3: Pola Candlestick
chapter3.description: Cara memprediksi pergerakan harga menggunakan pola candlestick.
chapter3.content_title: 7 Candlestick Pattern untuk Trading Saham yang Trader Perlu Tahu

candlestick_pattern.title: Candlestick Pattern
candlestick_pattern.description: A candlestick pattern is a graphical representation of price action that helps traders identify potential reversal or continuation opportunities.

paragraphs: The Marubozu pattern is characterized by a candle with a full body and no wick.
If the Marubozu candle forms with a closing price higher than the opening price, the price will likely rise the next day, and vice versa.
The strength of this candle can be determined by its volume; the greater the volume, the greater the strength of the candle.
A pattern shaped like a hammer and with a long wick (tail).
This pattern explains that during trading, the price fell, but rebounded and closed at nearly the same price as its opening.
This indicates strong buying demand, preventing the price from falling further.
This pattern can be considered strong or valid when the price is declining and supported by high volume.
Usually, after this pattern forms, there is potential for a reversal. Therefore, it is best to use this pattern in combination with identifying support.
This pattern is the opposite of the hammer pattern, more commonly known as the shooting star.
While buyers dominate in the hammer, sellers dominate in the inverted hammer.
This is because the price rose during trading hours, but then fell and closed at the same price as the opening.
This pattern is considered stronger or more valid when it forms during a price rally or uptrend supported by high volume.
Usually, after this pattern forms, the price reversal occurs, turning downward.
It's best to use this pattern in combination with resistance.
The doji pattern is characterized by a candlestick with no body, where the price during trading hours rises above or falls below the opening price, but closes at the opening price.
This pattern indicates a lack of dominant market power, either buyers or sellers.
However, when a doji forms after a significant stock price decline and is supported by volume, a reversal is likely.
Conversely, when a doji forms during a stock rally and is supported by high volume, a price decline is likely.

A bullish engulfing pattern is formed by two candlesticks, where the first candlestick indicates a price decline with a closing price lower than the opening price. The next day, the candlestick that forms covers the entire candlestick of the previous day with a closing price higher than the opening price.

The opposite of a bullish engulfing pattern is a bearish engulfing pattern, which occurs when the first candlestick rises, and the next day, the candlestick shows a price decline, covering the entire previous candlestick.
A bullish engulfing pattern indicates potential price movement upward, while a bearish engulfing pattern indicates potential price movement downward.
This pattern forms when a doji or hammer candlestick forms after the previous day's price decline.
If accompanied by high volume, there's a high potential for price increases.
This pattern forms if the price rose the previous day and a doji or hammer candlestick formed.
If accompanied by high volume, the price has a high potential to fall.
Harami, derived from the Japanese word "harami", means "pregnant", so this pattern resembles a mother carrying her child, seen from the side.
It begins with one large candle and is followed by a smaller candle the next day.

If the Harami pattern formation begins with a declining candle and is followed by a candle whose closing price is above the opening price, then the price is likely to rise the next day (Bullish Harami).
Conversely, if the formation begins with a rising candle and is followed by a candle whose closing price is below the opening price, then the price is likely to fall the next day (Bearish Harami).
In conclusion, candlestick patterns are used by traders as signals to predict stock price movements or the candlesticks that will form the following day.
Furthermore, the volume at the time of the candle's formation also plays a significant role in determining its validity.
The greater the volume, the more valid the candlestick pattern.

chapter4.title: Bab 4: Indikator
chapter4.description: Alat bantu untuk mendukung analisis teknikal.
chapter4.content_title: Cara Pakai Indikator Trading untuk Mencari Saham Bullish (MA, MACD, RSI, Fibonacci)

paragraphs: In addition to analyzing trends, patterns, and candlestick shapes, traders can also use trading indicators to aid technical analysis.
Why can indicators help improve a trader's analysis?
The reason is that indicators are created from mathematical calculations based on a stock's price.
However, traders should remember that indicators are only used as a tool to help predict future stock price movements, not as the sole and primary tool for traders to buy or sell stocks.
Indicators are basically divided into two types: lagging and leading indicators.
A lagging indicator is an indicator that appears late. This means it provides a buy or sell signal after the stock price has already moved.
It's suitable for swing traders who tend to be passive or rarely engage in buying and selling activities, and is more suitable for mid- to long-term traders.
Because it tends to be slower, this indicator provides more solid confirmation and accuracy.
A moving average is a line indicator that shows the average price over a specific period.
Moving averages can be presented in various time periods, for example, MA5, MA10, MA20, MA50, and even MA200.
The number after the MA indicates the selected time period.
Example: MA5 represents the average stock price over the last 5 days.

tutorial.title: How to Use Moving Average (MA) Indicator

text: Step by step:
points: Select 'Moving Average' from the list of indicators.
By default, a yellow line will appear, as shown in the image below.

function.title: Function of Moving Average (MA) Indicator
paragraphs: The Moving Average indicator can be used to help us identify stock price trends.
If the stock price is above the MA line, the price trend is up.
In practice, if the price is above the 200-day MA, the stock is considered to be in a strong uptrend.
Furthermore, the MA line can also be used as support and resistance.

examples.title: Role of Moving Average (MA) as Support and Resistance

text: Support: Price above the moving average (MA) line
Resistance: Price below the moving average (MA) line
MA200 is one of the most frequently used MAs to assess a trend as an uptrend or downtrend.
In 2016, when BBCA rebounded and began to correct, it was stuck in the 200-day moving average (MA200).
Similarly, in 2018, when BBCA corrected, it briefly traded in the 200-day moving average (MA200). This also occurred in October 2018 and again in May 2019.
When the COVID-19 virus entered Indonesia, the 200-day moving average (MA200) was broken, and BBCA subsequently moved bearish to 4200.

examples.title: Role of Moving Average (MA) as Buy/Sell Signal

text: MA20: blue
MA50: black
Buying Signal: If the small MA line (MA20) penetrates the MA50 area from bottom to top, this moment is called as "golden cross".
Selling Signal: If the small MA line (MA20) penetrates the MA50 area from top to bottom, this moment is called as "death cross".

definition.title: What is MACD?
definition.p1: MACD is a derivative indicator of Moving Average which stands for Moving Average Convergence Divergence.

list.text: In the MACD indicator there are several important components that must be considered, namely:

example.title: Example of using the MACD indicator

text: Step by step:
Buying Signal: If the signal line (black) is penetrated by the MACD line (red) from bottom to top, this moment is called as "golden cross".
Selling Signal: If the signal line (black) is penetrated by the MACD line (red) from top to bottom, this moment is called a "death cross".

content.connecting_text_1: In addition to the above techniques, there is an additional technique in using the MACD indicator, namely divergence.

definition_macd_divergence.title: What is MACD Divergence?
paragraphs: As the name suggests, divergence means difference.
So, MACD Divergence is a technique that identifies differences between the direction of stock price movement and the MACD indicator.
The stock price shows an uptrend with the formation of continuously higher highs, but the MACD indicator declines below it.
This phenomenon is also known as MACD bearish divergence and is a bear signal, or a signal to sell the stock.

example_macd_bearish_divergence.title: MACD Bearish Divergence Has Occurred in the Following Stock

text: When INCO's stock price successfully creates a higher high for up to three candles, something different happens in its MACD, where the MACD crossover point actually decreases, creating a divergence, which is a sell signal.
Furthermore, when a bearish divergence occurs, INCO's stock price is unable to continue its upward movement and enters a bearish phase.

content.connecting_text_2: Besides bearish divergence, there is also something called bullish divergence.

definition_macd_bullish_divergence.title: What is MACD Bullish Divergence?
paragraphs: This is the opposite of bearish divergence, where the stock price shows a downward trend. The candlesticks form increasingly lower lows.
However, the MACD indicator trend below shows an upward trend. Conversely, there is divergence.

example_macd_bullish_divergence.title: MACD Bullish Divergence Has Occurred in the Following Stocks

text: INCO successfully created a lower low, or a lower price movement, but there was a difference in the strength of the MACD. The MACD crossover point rose, creating a divergence, which signaled a buy signal.
This was a reversal moment, a sign that INCO would soon continue its price increase and end the bearish phase.

definition.title: What is Bollinger Bands?
paragraphs: Bollinger Bands is a trading indicator that is formed from three components, namely:

points.text: A moving average line (usually the 20-day MA), which represents the mean or average price over a specific period.

paragraphs: This indicator is used to monitor the volatility of a stock price.
The wider the distance between the upper and lower bands, the greater the volatility.
Furthermore, we can use the lower band as support and the upper band as resistance.
The moving average (MA) line acts as a reference area or benchmark, indicating the high probability that the price will return to that level if it fails to break through the lower or upper band.

examples.title: Example of Using Bollinger Bands Indicator as Support and Resistance

text: Step by step:
points: Select 'Bollinger Bands' from the list of indicators.
The correct Bollinger Bands use MA20 (already set by default)

text: Simply put, Bollinger Bands create a cloud or demand area and subline.
If the price successfully breaks through the upper Bollinger Band area, it will tend to re-enter the cloud area, and vice versa.
When SIDO breaks through the lower Bollinger Band area, the price then rebounds back into the Bollinger Band area.
When it breaks through the upper Bollinger Band area again, the price re-enters.

paragraphs: Besides being used as support and resistance, Bollinger Bands can also be used to identify changes or trends.
A new trend is likely to form when the distance between the previously relatively wide bands begins to narrow.
Leading indicators provide forecasts or price projections, signaling them before the price moves.
Suitable for short-term traders with high and frequent trading volumes.

definition.title: What is RSI?
paragraphs: The Relative Strength Index (RSI) analyzes the magnitude of price changes or volatility in a stock's price to evaluate whether the stock is overbought or oversold.
The RSI indicator ranges from 20 to 80. Essentially, if the RSI line reaches 80 or above, the stock is considered overbought.
Conversely, if the RSI line falls below 20, the stock is considered oversold.

text: Step by step:
points: Select 'Relative Strength Index' from the list of indicators.

text: When ITMG's stock price rises sharply and reaches the 80 area and begins to correct, it indicates a sell signal.
Furthermore, when ITMG's stock corrects and falls to the 20 area, it indicates an oversold area.
When it reaches the oversold area and the indicator begins to show an increase, it is a buy signal.
Afterward, the ITMG stock price can be seen to rise.
If the indicator subsequently shows a decrease, prepare to close your ITMG position.

paragraphs: In practice, the RSI can also be used in combination with chart patterns.
For example, a stock price forms a bullish chart pattern, accompanied by the RSI rising from 20.
This can be considered a stronger buy signal because it is supported by two signals: the chart pattern and the leading indicator.

definition.title: What is Fibonacci Retracement?
paragraphs: Fibonacci retracement is a tool that can be used to gain momentum.
The Fibonacci sequence is used in various disciplines such as biology, astronomy, architecture, geology, and finance.
Fibonacci is commonly used to find rebounds or corrections in a stock, using the golden ratio points on the Fibonacci as support or resistance.
Fibonacci is created by drawing a range from the highest point to the lowest point, or vice versa, from the lowest point to the highest point.
When the Fibonacci line is drawn, several golden ratio points will automatically form, which can be used as support and resistance levels.

text: Step by step:
points: Select the menu with the '4 strip' icon to the left of the chart.

text: In BBRI stock, when you look at the lowest point on May 18, 2020, and the highest point on August 25, 2020, when the BBRI stock price began to correct, we're certainly confused about what price to enter.
Well, we can use Fibonacci because it can assess momentum.
When we draw a line from the lowest point to the highest point, we can see that BBRI successfully rebounded at three key Fibonacci levels.
Namely, at 38.20%, 50.00%, or 61.80% (These three points are the three key Fibonacci levels).
In this case, BBRI rebounded from the 50.00% level and continued its price increase.
Fibonacci can also be used to indicate a rebound in stocks experiencing a downtrend.
Rebounds typically occur at the 0.618 or 61.8% Fibonacci level.
To find the target point, we draw a line from the top of the price down.
BBNI's highest point before the pandemic outbreak on January 23, 2020, was 4670.
Then, BBNI began to rebound to its lowest point on May 18, 2020.
When it begins to rebound, we want to determine the price it can rebound to.
To find its potential price, we can use Fibonacci.
We'll draw a line from the highest price to the lowest. Here, Fibonacci provides the highest area or golden ratio for BBRI at 61.80, or 3700.
Note that BBRI was temporarily held in this area, creating a supply and demand area.
This area is resistance when it hasn't been broken through and becomes support when it has been broken through.

paragraphs: In conclusion, trading indicators are categorized into two categories: lagging and leading.
The role of indicators is to help traders predict price movements and can be combined with other analysis tools such as trends, chart patterns, and volume.
Many indicators are available, allowing traders to trial and error and choose the indicator that best suits their trading style.

chapter5.title: Bab 5: Taktik Trading dengan Analisis Teknikal
chapter5.description: Tips & trik kombinasi pola, volume, dan indikator.
chapter5.content_title: Taktik Trading Saham Supaya Cuan Maksimal

text: Strategy for buying shares when the price falls near support

paragraphs: This strategy is usually used on stocks that are still in an uptrend, but are undergoing a natural correction or are being hit by profit-taking.
For example, this strategy can be used when the price is in an uptrend and forming higher highs or higher lows.
Traders can buy when the price forms higher lows along a trendline that acts as support.

text: As can be seen, this stock is in an uptrend, touching the support area.
The stock price touched the lowest level in the uptrend but failed to break through the support area and continue its uptrend.
Therefore, when this happens, traders can use it as an opportunity to take a buy position.
Strategy for buying shares when the price breaks through resistance

paragraphs: Traders who use this technique hope to sell their stock at a higher price, buying high and selling higher.
The advantage of this tactic is that traders usually don't have to wait long for the stock to move higher.
However, it's not uncommon for the stock to fall back below the newly broken resistance level, a phenomenon known as a false breakout.

text: Here, ISAT successfully broke through the neckline of the inverted head and shoulders pattern, which also acts as resistance at the neckline.
Traders with a buy-on-breakout strategy will buy at a price above the neckline.
Strategy to sell when the price experiences a trend reversal

paragraphs: The advantage of using this strategy is that traders can follow the overall trend in stocks, thereby maximizing profits on stocks that are experiencing an upward trend in the long term.

text: In the example above, the price continued to fall as the stock formed a support area.
Traders using a sell-on-breakdown strategy would sell when the price broke below the support area.
Strategy to sell when the price approaches the resistance line

paragraphs: This strategy is very suitable for use on stocks that move sideways.

content.p1: In this chapter, we have learned four buy and sell strategies: Buy on Weakness, Buy on Breakout, Sell on Breakdown, and Sell on Strength.

highlight.title: Common Trading Technique Pairs
text: Buy on Weakness + Sell on Strength
Buy on Breakout + Sell on Breakdown

content.p2: Using this strategy, we can determine the appropriate trading tactics.
